title: rosa chinensis borbonica
intro: Rosa chinensis borbonica, also known as the Rose of China or Chinese Rose, is a flowering plant native to China and introduced to the island of Réunion, with its name being attributed to the Bourbon family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which aid in soothing skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Rosa chinensis borbonica include reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with menopause. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
